brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +36.2% since Feb 27 — largest single-day drop of war (−3.5%) on ceasefire-plan headlines; partial reversal after Iran rejection
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 25 (Wed): $96.68 close, down 3.5% — largest single-day drop since war began. Intraday sank ~7% to near $93 on ceasefire-plan headlines before recovering after Iran rejected the 15-point proposal as 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' Now ~$17 below $113.41 war-peak (Mar 10-12). Goldman Sachs: 'largest shock in decades as share of global supply.' IEA cut demand growth by 210 kb/d; 400M bbl reserves released. Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +33.8% since Feb 27 — fell 2.7-3.6% on ceasefire plan; $89.01 close per CNBC
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 25 (Wed): $89.01 close (CNBC), down ~2.7-3.6% from prev. Ceasefire-plan headlines drove prices lower before partial recovery after Iran rejection at ~8 AM ET. Gas at ~$3.96/gal national average (up 10 consecutive weeks, +$1.02 in March, +41% YTD). California $5.62/gal. Source: NYMEX / CNBC / AAA.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$0.98 / +32.9% since Feb 27 — ~$3.96 AAA avg — up 10 consecutive weeks — +$1.02 in March — +41% YTD — CA $5.62
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 25: ~$3.96/gal (+32.9% since war began). Up 10 consecutive weeks. +$1.02 in March alone. +41% YTD. California $5.62/gal (highest). AP-NORC poll: 45% extremely/very concerned about gas affordability. 59% say war has 'gone too far.' Source: AAA / AP-NORC / CNBC.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -283 pts / -4.1% — +0.6% on ceasefire plan + oil plunge; all sectors green except energy; approaching 200-day MA
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 25 (Wed): ~6,596 (+0.6%). Second day of rebound. Pre-market futures surged >1% on 15-point ceasefire plan, cut in half after Iran rejection at 8 AM ET, recovered through session. Approached but did not breach 200-day MA (~6,624). All 11 sectors green except energy producers. Dow ~46,454 (+0.72%). Nasdaq ~21,985 (+0.92%). ARM +15%, AMD/Intel +5% each. VIX 26.3 (−0.65). Source: NYSE / CNBC / Bloomberg.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — 9,000+ targets — 9,000+ sorties — 82nd Airborne deploying — $200B supplemental proposed — power plant pause deadline ~Mar 28
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 26: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. 82nd Airborne deployment confirmed — HQ, division enablers, and 1st BCT deploying to CENTCOM. Pentagon sent White House $200B supplemental funding proposal. Speaker Johnson: 'Operation Epic Fury is almost done.' Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military ($65B most likely), $50-210B economic ($115B most likely). Replenishment cost $3.1B+ and rising ~$758M/day (CSIS). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (26 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$30.3-30.8B — $200B supplemental proposed
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 26: ~$30.3-30.8B. Pentagon sent $200B supplemental to White House. Speaker Johnson says 'probably' part of reconciliation bill. Penn Wharton full-war projection: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic losses. Replenishment: $3.1B+ (~$758M/day). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 9,000+ US — 9,000+ combat flights — 130+ vessels — ~27,000+ combined est. — IDF: wide-scale attacks on Tehran govt infrastructure
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 25): 9,000+ US targets, 9,000+ combat flights confirmed (CBS). IDF launched 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure including Tehran, Qazvin, Isfahan submarine research center. South Tehran strike killed 12, wounded 28. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~27,000+ through Day 26. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / CBS News.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 7 KIA combat + 6 KC-135 crash + 1 health-related — ~140 WIA (8 severely)
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 7 KIA combat: 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2), 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13, non-combat). 1 health-related death. Total: 13-14 KIA. ~140 WIA (8 severely) per Al Jazeera tracker. 82nd Airborne deploying to CENTCOM (~1,000-2,000 troops). Source: CENTCOM / AP / Al Jazeera.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: ~140 total — 8 severely wounded — 50,000+ deployed + 2,000 82nd ABN incoming
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. ~140 WIA (8 severely) per Al Jazeera tracker. 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR plus 2,000 82nd Airborne ordered. Source: CENTCOM / Al Jazeera / Pentagon.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 1,500 killed / 18,551 wounded — Red Crescent: 1,200+ — Hengaw thru Day 18: 5,300 (511 civ / 4,789 mil) — Newsweek: 1,348+
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. Iran Health Ministry: 1,500 killed, 18,551 wounded. Red Crescent: 1,200+ killed. Hengaw (thru Day 18): 5,300 killed (511 civilian, 4,789 military). Newsweek aggregated: 1,348+. Infrastructure: 5,535+ residential units, 1,041 commercial, 14 medical centers, 65 schools, 25 hospitals damaged (9 out of service), 13 Red Crescent centers. 55 healthcare workers wounded, 11 killed. Aggregate cross-theater: ~2,600-7,500+ killed. Source: Iran Health Ministry / Red Crescent / Hengaw / Newsweek / Britannica.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,001-1,072 killed — 2,584-2,966 wounded — 33 killed last 24 hrs — 1.0-1.2M displaced (1 in 5-6 Lebanese)
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and Hezbollah operations since March 2. 1,001-1,072 killed (Lebanese Health Ministry / NBC). 2,584-2,966 wounded. 33 killed in last 24 hrs. 1.0-1.2 million displaced (1 in 5-6 Lebanese). Israel declared 'security zone' south of Litani (~30km) — DM Katz says displaced 'will not return.' All bridges over Litani destroyed. Hezbollah chief Qassem vows to fight 'without limits.' Hezbollah fires 100+ rockets at Israeli positions. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / NBC / NPR / UNHCR.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.2M+ across Iran, Lebanon, Afghanistan, Pakistan — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 400 in Gulf of Oman — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: 4.2M+ internally displaced across Afghanistan, Iran, Lebanon, and Pakistan. Iran: 3.2M (UNHCR). Lebanon: 1M+ (~19% of pop). 125,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria. 3,000+ vessels stranded (IMO). ~400 vessels backed up in Gulf of Oman (Windward). 40,000+ sailors stranded. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IMO / Windward.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 40,000+ sailors — 21+ vessel attacks — 7+ seafarer deaths
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28 (Travel Tourister/Cirium). 3,000+ vessels stranded in Middle East (IMO via NPR Mar 21). 40,000+ seafarers stranded. ~150 ships anchored outside Hormuz, ~170 containerships (450,000 TEU) trapped inside Gulf. 15,000+ cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 21+ maritime attacks, 7+ seafarer fatalities. JMIC/UKMTO: CRITICAL. Insurance at 5% of vessel value. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 210+ (Health Min) + HRANA 214 + Lebanon 118+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — 2,100+ killed or injured — UNICEF
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed. HRANA (Mar 23): 214 children confirmed killed in Iran. Lebanon: 118+ children (UNICEF). Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. UNICEF (Mar 17): 2,100+ children killed or injured total across region. 324 children killed (206 Iran, 118 Lebanon). Source: UNICEF / Iran Health Min / HRANA / Lebanese MoPH.
